findings.claim: ggpaintr has two parallel source->consumer implementations — single-instance (ptr_setup_source_uis + ptr_setup_consumer_uis, real state) and shared-coordinator (ptr_setup_panel_sources + ptr_bind_shared_consumer_uis); conflating them is the primary way to misread the code.
findings.detail: Single-instance always has non-NULL state; shared HOST scope runs state=NULL (ADR 0006). The per-instance source binder skips panel-owned ids so they render once at host scope.
findings.verification: Four named functions present and distinct at HEAD f5a367c: ptr_setup_source_uis (server.R:2297), ptr_setup_consumer_uis (2659), ptr_setup_panel_sources (shared-ui.R:510), ptr_bind_shared_consumer_uis (server.R:2948); comments flag them as kept 'in lockstep'/'byte-stable'.

findings.claim: ptr_bind_shared_consumer_uis runs in two modes from one body — host (state=NULL, top-level pickers) and per-instance (state!=NULL, inside each embedded ptr_server) — so any change must be gated on is.null(state).
findings.detail: Host call relies on the state=NULL default arg. The ab4b6dd latch is wrapped in `if (is.null(state))`.
findings.verification: Confirmed one body, two modes gated on is.null(state): host (default state=NULL server.R:2955; host-only blocks 3115, 3297) vs per-instance (if(!is.null(state)) ... req(state$bound_names) server.R:3165-3226); explicit comment 'gated on is.null(state)' at 3004-3005.

findings.claim: consumer_upstream_source_state builds identity = sid#datapath#boundName, but at host scope (state=NULL) boundName is always "" (no state$bound_names), so the host consumer identity is datapath-only.
findings.detail: bn = tryCatch({ r <- state$bound_names[[sid]]; ... r() }, error=""); NULL$bound_names -> NULL[[sid]] -> NULL -> bn "". Root cause of both the double-render revert and the env-rename iceberg.
findings.verification: Confirmed: identity=paste0(sid,'#',dp,'#',bn) at server.R:2621; bn derives from state$bound_names inside tryCatch(...,error=->'') (2596-2615), so at host (state=NULL) bn is always '' and identity is datapath-only; in-code comment 2998-2999 states exactly this.

findings.claim: At host scope, uploading over a typed source now DOES clear and stay cleared: the base identity clear trips on beat 1 but beat 2's identity is stable (bn=''), so a cleared_for_identity/held_clear latch (R/paintr-server.R:3297-3313) re-asserts the clear on the upload's trailing render — the earlier 'beat-2 reverts the clear' iceberg is fixed.
findings.detail: Single-instance is immune because state!=NULL makes bn change across beats. Worked around in ab4b6dd by a host-scope cleared_for_identity latch. Probe: dev/scratch/ppupload7-probes.
findings.verification: DRIFT: the described iceberg is now FIXED. Verified the host-scope latch exists — held_clear=!is.null(cleared_for_identity) && identical(cleared_for_identity,src_state$identity) && current_nonempty; clear_for_new_source<-clear||held_clear||vacate_edge (server.R:3300-3311). Claim rewritten to reflect the fix.

findings.claim: ICEBERG partially open: a host env->env shortcut rename is still not cleared by identity detection (datapath '' + host bn '' -> constant 'shared_ds##', server.R:2621/2653-2654); the new host vacate_edge latch (server.R:3303) only catches a supply->no-supply edge, not a non-default->non-default env rename, so this specific case remains unclosed and untested.
findings.detail: The ab4b6dd latch cannot help (it only re-asserts an existing clear; base_clear never trips). Real fix = plan's optional bound_name_for= param on consumer_upstream_source_state (skipped). Confirm with a host env->env-rename e2e using a column in BOTH frames (body_mass_g) — unique columns are auto-dropped by selectInput (false green).
findings.verification: DRIFT: partially addressed but NOT fully closed. consumer_clear_for_new_source.changed_since_last still goes FALSE for env->env rename (constant identity 'shared_ds##', server.R:2621/2653-2654); the new vacate_edge (server.R:3303) only catches a supply->no-supply edge, and user_supplied stays TRUE across a non-default->non-default rename (2623), so the rename case remains open. Claim rewritten.

findings.claim: resolve_upload_source(..., file_reset=FALSE) already had the param; when TRUE it forces file_info<-NULL, routing to the env-load + vacate-on-empty branches regardless of the stale server-side fileInput value.
findings.detail: A re-rendered fileInput can't report its cleared state, so input[[src_id]] stays stale; file_reset is the authoritative 'display is gone' signal. The #7 follow-up only feeds this arg from the host path.
findings.verification: Confirmed: resolve_upload_source(...,file_reset=FALSE) signature at server.R:1300-1301; if(isTRUE(file_reset)) file_info<-NULL at 1337 routes to env-load (try_bind_source_default_resolved, 1350) + vacate-on-empty (vacate_source_binding, 1374).

findings.claim: On the host source path source_file_reset can be a plain local reactiveVal read directly at the in-scope resolve_upload_source call, because the renderUI, flag observers, and resolve observe all live in one local() per source key — unlike the single-instance path which must publish on state$source_file_reset.
findings.verification: Confirmed: file_reset_rv reactiveVal local to the per-key local() in ptr_setup_panel_sources (shared-ui.R:575), read directly at the resolve call (683); single-instance publishes state$source_file_reset (server.R:2349). Contrast comment verbatim at shared-ui.R:570-573.

findings.claim: ptr_bind_source_mutex only does file->text (an observeEvent on the fileInput that blanks the shortcut textInput); it does NOT clear the file when the user types, so the typed-over-upload visual clear depends on the #7 rising-edge re-render, not the mutex.
findings.verification: Confirmed: ptr_bind_source_mutex (server.R:1671-1678) has one observeEvent(input[[src_id]]) that updateTextInput(...,value='') (file->blank shortcut); no reverse observer. Typed-over-upload visual clear is the ADR-0025 #7 source_bump re-render (server.R:2355-2362), not the mutex.

findings.claim: The fileInput filename pill is rendered client-side by the browser's native file widget, so chromote/CDP get_html/get_value cannot observe it — #7's pill-clear is only verifiable by human eyeball, never by AppDriver.
findings.detail: Chrome MCP also now rejects host filesystem paths in file_upload and the native picker is undrivable, so even a screenshot agent cannot create the upload pill to watch it clear; typing->re-render->env-load->picker-populated IS automatable.
findings.verification: Durable harness fact: the fileInput filename pill is rendered by the browser's native file widget and is NOT observable via chromote/CDP get_html/get_value, so any pill-clear success criterion must be a human eyeball, never an AppDriver assertion. (Applies to both stamped instances.)

findings.claim: Adding file_reset= to the host resolve_upload_source call broke test-auto-name-shared-canonical.R because its local_mocked_bindings mock used the old 8-arg signature (no file_reset, no ...), so the call threw 'unused argument', the observe errored, captured_node stayed NULL, and 3 expectations failed — a stale-mock failure, not a product bug.
findings.detail: Lesson: when adding an argument to a function mocked via testthat::local_mocked_bindings, grep tests for mocks of that function and update their signatures (or add ...). Fixed by adding file_reset=FALSE to the mock.
findings.verification: Confirmed in tests/testthat/test-auto-name-shared-canonical.R:78-90: the local_mocked_bindings mock for resolve_upload_source now mirrors the real signature including file_reset=FALSE, with a comment documenting that the host path (ptr_setup_panel_sources) passes file_reset= and the mock must accept it or captured_node stays NULL. Mechanism and fix confirmed.

findings.claim: On branch ppUpload-bug the serial full-suite authoritative gate was PASS 3403 / FAIL 0 / WARN 4 / SKIP 0 before this change and PASS 3420 after (the 17 new test-shared-host-source-reset assertions); WARN 4 is pre-existing and 0/0/0 is the gate, not the count.
findings.detail: Point-in-time campaign fact; PASS count drifts as tests are added.
findings.source: devtools::test() serial; project CLAUDE.md authoritative-gate block
findings.verification: Point-in-time test-count observation on the (now-merged) ppUpload-bug branch with no durable source ('observed this session'); cannot be re-verified, and CLAUDE.md states the count is explicitly NOT the gate (0/0/0 is). Kept live but flagged — a human should decide whether to retire it.

findings.claim: Tool results were sometimes delivered in delayed batches — a result for call N appeared one or more turns later, occasionally with a truncated/garbled tail — which can be mistaken for file corruption but is purely a delivery artifact; every read reconciled to clean content on the batched flush.
findings.detail: Mitigation: keep firing the needed reads and reconcile on flush; prefer serena symbolic reads (find_symbol include_body=true) which returned clean complete bodies. Recurred during this very harvest (a grep -c printed duplicated many times).
findings.verification: Session-environment observation (delayed batched tool-result delivery, not file corruption) with no source ref; not re-verifiable against code. Kept live but flagged.
